milestones.title: The Turing Test
milestones.description: Alan Turing published 'Computing Machinery and Intelligence', proposing the famous Turing Test to determine if a machine can exhibit intelligent behavior indistinguishable from a human.
milestones.impact: Defined the foundational question of AI: can machines think?

milestones.title: Birth of AI — Dartmouth Conference
milestones.description: John McCarthy coined the term 'Artificial Intelligence' at the Dartmouth Conference, marking the official birth of AI as a field of study.
milestones.impact: Established AI as a formal academic discipline.

milestones.title: The Perceptron
milestones.description: Frank Rosenblatt invented the Perceptron, the first artificial neural network capable of learning from data.
milestones.impact: Laid the groundwork for modern neural networks and deep learning.

milestones.title: ELIZA — First Chatbot
milestones.description: Joseph Weizenbaum created ELIZA, one of the first programs to simulate conversation between a human and a machine using pattern matching.
milestones.impact: Demonstrated that humans could be tricked into believing they were talking to a person.

milestones.title: Shakey the Robot
milestones.description: Shakey was the first general-purpose mobile robot that could reason about its own actions, combining robotics and AI planning.
milestones.impact: Pioneered the integration of perception, reasoning, and action in robotics.

milestones.title: First AI Winter
milestones.description: Funding and interest in AI research dropped significantly due to unmet expectations and criticism of progress, entering a period known as the 'AI Winter'.
milestones.impact: Forced researchers to focus on more realistic and achievable goals.

milestones.title: Expert Systems Boom
milestones.description: Expert systems like XCON became commercially successful, using rule-based AI to solve complex domain-specific problems.
milestones.impact: Brought AI into real business applications for the first time.

milestones.title: Backpropagation Rediscovered
milestones.description: Geoffrey Hinton popularized the backpropagation algorithm, enabling neural networks with multiple layers to learn effectively.
milestones.impact: Revived neural network research and eventually led to deep learning.

milestones.title: Deep Blue Beats Kasparov
milestones.description: IBM's Deep Blue became the first computer to defeat a reigning world chess champion, Garry Kasparov, in a match under standard chess tournament conditions.
milestones.impact: Proved that AI could surpass human performance in complex strategic games.

milestones.title: Deep Learning Revolution
milestones.description: Geoffrey Hinton introduced deep belief networks, showing that deep neural networks could be trained efficiently, kickstarting the deep learning era.
milestones.impact: Transformed AI research and led to breakthroughs in vision, speech, and language.

milestones.title: IBM Watson Wins Jeopardy!
milestones.description: IBM's Watson defeated human champions on the game show Jeopardy!, demonstrating advanced natural language processing and knowledge retrieval.
milestones.impact: Showed AI's ability to understand and process natural human language at scale.

milestones.title: AlexNet Wins ImageNet
milestones.description: AlexNet, a deep convolutional neural network, dramatically outperformed all other models in the ImageNet competition, revolutionizing computer vision.
milestones.impact: Sparked the modern deep learning era and changed how we approach image recognition.

milestones.title: AlphaGo Defeats Lee Sedol
milestones.description: Google DeepMind's AlphaGo defeated world Go champion Lee Sedol 4-1, a feat previously thought to be decades away due to the game's complexity.
milestones.impact: Demonstrated that AI could master intuition-based games far beyond human capability.

milestones.title: Transformer Architecture
milestones.description: Google researchers published 'Attention Is All You Need', introducing the Transformer architecture that powers most modern AI language models.
milestones.impact: Became the foundation for GPT, BERT, and virtually every major language model today.

milestones.title: GPT-3 Released
milestones.description: OpenAI released GPT-3, a language model with 175 billion parameters capable of generating human-like text, code, and answering questions.
milestones.impact: Showed that large language models could perform a wide range of tasks without specific training.

milestones.title: ChatGPT Launch
milestones.description: OpenAI launched ChatGPT, reaching 1 million users in just 5 days and sparking global conversation about the role of AI in everyday life.
milestones.impact: Made AI accessible to the general public and triggered an AI adoption wave worldwide.

milestones.title: Multimodal AI Goes Mainstream
milestones.description: Models like GPT-4, Gemini, and Claude gained the ability to process both text and images, enabling richer and more versatile AI interactions.
milestones.impact: Expanded AI use cases into design, medicine, education, and beyond.
